subject: Science
question_text: A fire alarm usually detects smoke in case of fire. Where should such an alarm be placed in a room?
answer_key: On the ceiling
answer_explanation: Smoke is warmer and lighter than the surrounding air, so it rises upwards towards the ceiling.
provenance_excerpt: A fire alarm usually detects smoke in case of fire. Where should such an alarm be placed in a room?

question_text: Four schematic diagrams are shown in Figure 8.1 to depict the direction of sea breeze. Which of them gives the correct direction?
answer_explanation: Sea breeze blows from the relatively cooler sea towards the warmer land during the daytime.

subject: Science
question_text: Figure 8.2 shows a child blowing air with a straw near the opening of another straw which has its other end in a soft drink bottle. It was observed that the level of the soft drink in the straw rises up as soon as air is blown over its open end. Which one of the following best explains the reason for rise in level of the drink?
options: Blowing of air decreases pressure over the opening of the straw.
The straw of the soft drink bottle collapses when air is blown over its open end.
Blowing of air warms up the air inside the straw.
Blowing of air increases the pressure on the surface of soft drink in the bottle.
answer_key: Blowing of air decreases pressure over the opening of the straw.
answer_explanation: According to Bernoulli's principle, increased wind speed decreases air pressure. The higher pressure inside the bottle pushes the liquid up.

subject: Science
question_text:
Following are precautions one must take in case a storm is accompanied by lightning.
(i) Do not take shelter under a tree.
(ii) Do not take shelter under an umbrella with a metallic end.
(iii) Do not take shelter in open garages, storage sheds, etc.
(iv) Do not take shelter in a bus in the open.
Which one of these is not correct?
answer_key: (iv)
answer_explanation: Taking shelter inside a bus or car is relatively safe during a lightning storm, provided the windows are closed, as the metal body acts as a Faraday cage.

subject: Science
question_text: Which of the following place is most likely to be affected by a cyclone?
answer_key: Puri
answer_explanation: Puri is situated on the eastern coast of India, which is highly vulnerable to cyclones originating in the Bay of Bengal.
provenance_excerpt: Which of the following place is most likely to be affected by a cyclone?

subject: Science
question_text: A curtain is hanging at the entrance of a room. A long corridor runs at right angles to the door, that is parallel to the curtain. If a strong wind blows along the corridor, the curtain will
answer_key: get pushed outside the room.
answer_explanation: Strong wind along the corridor reduces the air pressure outside the room. The higher pressure inside the room will push the curtain outside.

subject: Science
question_text: Why is Chandigarh unlikely to be affected by a cyclone?
answer_key: Chandigarh is located far away from the ocean/coastline. Cyclones originate over warm seas and lose their strength quickly when they hit land.
answer_explanation: Geographical vulnerability to cyclones.
provenance_excerpt: Why is Chandigarh unlikely to be affected by a cyclone?

subject: Science
question_text: Name the ocean which is mainly responsible to bring rain bearing monsoon winds to Kerala coast in June every year?
answer_key: Indian Ocean (Arabian Sea branch).
answer_explanation: Geography related to monsoon winds.
provenance_excerpt: Name the ocean which is mainly responsible to bring rain bearing monsoon winds to Kerala coast in June every year?

subject: Science
question_text:
State whether the following statements are True or False:
(i) If wind flows from land to the ocean, then it is daytime.
(ii) A very high pressure system, with very high speed wind surrounding it forms a cyclone.
(iii) The coast line of India is not vulnerable to cyclones.
(iv) Warm air is lighter than cool air.
answer_key:
(i) False (Land breeze occurs at night)
(ii) False (A cyclone is a low pressure system)
(iii) False (The eastern coast is highly vulnerable)
(iv) True
answer_explanation: Validating statements on wind and cyclones.
provenance_excerpt:
State whether the following statements are True or False:
(i) If wind flows from land to the ocean, then it is daytime.

subject: Science
question_text: To expel hot air out of the kitchen, ‘A’ has an exhaust fan fitted on the window of her kitchen and ‘B’ has a similar exhaust fan fitted on the wall near the ceiling of her kitchen. Which of the exhaust fan will expel the hot air more effectively? Explain why?
answer_key: Exhaust fan 'B' will expel hot air more effectively. Hot air expands, becomes lighter, and rises up towards the ceiling.
answer_explanation: Application of convection currents of air.

subject: Science
question_text: Why is it advisable not to shut all the doors and windows during a storm?
answer_key: Strong winds blowing outside reduce the air pressure. If all doors and windows are shut, the higher air pressure inside may push outward strongly, potentially blowing off the roof or breaking windows.
answer_explanation: Understanding Bernoulli's principle in real situations.
provenance_excerpt: Why is it advisable not to shut all the doors and windows during a storm?

subject: Science
question_text: A flat in Mumbai with a balcony facing the sea has some clothes hung on a clothes line in the balcony. Towards which direction the clothes will be blown in the afternoon? Explain.
answer_key: The clothes will be blown towards the inside of the house (away from the sea). During the afternoon (daytime), the sea breeze blows from the sea towards the land.
answer_explanation: Application of sea breeze concept.

subject: Science
question_text: Figure 8.3 shows a diagrammatic representation of trees in the afternoon along a sea coast. State on which side is the sea; A or B? Give reasons for your choice.
answer_key: The sea is on side B (if the trees are bent towards A) or vice versa depending on the figure. During the afternoon, sea breeze blows from sea to land, bending the trees towards the land.
answer_explanation: Inferring wind direction from physical effects.

question_text: A flag mounted on a flag post near the sea coast flutters in the direction of sea. At what time of the day does this happen – at midnight or in the afternoon?
answer_key: This happens at midnight (or late night). The land breeze blows from the land towards the sea during the night.

subject: Science
question_text: Paheli kept an empty bottle made of plastic inside a refrigerator. After few hours, when she opened the refrigerator she found the bottle had collapsed. Explain the possible reason.
answer_key: The air inside the bottle cooled down, contracted and its pressure decreased. The higher atmospheric pressure from outside crushed (collapsed) the bottle.
answer_explanation: Air pressure and temperature relationship.
provenance_excerpt: Paheli kept an empty bottle made of plastic inside a refrigerator. After few hours, when she opened the refrigerator she

subject: Science
question_text: When strong/high speed wind blows, an umbrella held upright at times gets upturned. Explain the reason.
answer_key: High speed wind moving over the umbrella creates an area of low pressure above it. The higher pressure below the umbrella pushes it upwards, causing it to upturn.
answer_explanation: Application of Bernoulli's principle to everyday objects.
provenance_excerpt: When strong/high speed wind blows, an umbrella held upright at times gets upturned. Explain the reason.

subject: Science
question_text: Suggest some precautions to be taken to prevent the roof of a tin sheet from flying away during a fierce wind storm.
answer_key:
1. Put heavy stones or sandbags on the roof.
2. Fasten the tin sheets tightly with screws or strong ropes to the heavy structural beams.
answer_explanation: Practical safety during windstorms.
provenance_excerpt: Suggest some precautions to be taken to prevent the roof of a tin sheet from flying away during a fierce wind storm.

subject: Science
question_text: Describe an activity to demonstrate that warm air is lighter than cool air.
answer_key: Take two paper bags of the same size and hang them inverted at the two ends of a wooden stick. Balance the stick by a thread tied to its centre. Place a burning candle below one of the bags. The bag above the candle will rise. This happens because the warm air rises and pushes the bag up, showing that warm air is lighter than cool air.
answer_explanation: Experimental design for air properties.
provenance_excerpt: Describe an activity to demonstrate that warm air is lighter than cool air.

subject: Science
question_text: The picture in Figure 8.4 shows tree line along the sea coast on an island near the equator. As shown, the tree tops are permanently bent in one direction. Are the trees bent towards the sea or away from it? Explain.
answer_key: They are bent away from the sea. Near the equator coastlines, strong sea breezes regularly blow from the sea towards the land for most of the day, causing the trees to permanently bend in the direction of the wind (landwards).
answer_explanation: Observation of chronic environmental impacts.
